title: Mass marketing and niche marketing
summary: 9609 AS — mass vs niche strategies, advantages, disadvantages, and choosing for firm size and market.
learningObjectives: Compare advantages and disadvantages of each.
Link to segmentation and the marketing mix.

simpleExplanation.title: Everyone or someone specific
simpleExplanation.summary: Mass marketing targets the whole market with one offer; niche marketing focuses on a small, well-defined segment with tailored products and messages.
steps: Mass: economies of scale, high promotion spend.
Mass suits large firms and universal products.
Niche suits specialists and small businesses.

flashcards.back: Single marketing mix aimed at entire market — undifferentiated approach.
Focused on small segment with specialised needs — differentiated offer.
Economies of scale, lower unit costs, wide recognition.
High ad spend, less personal, ignored by some segments.
Less direct competition, premium prices, strong loyalty, expert reputation.
Limited market size, vulnerable if niche disappears or big firm enters.
Niche marketing follows segmentation — target one segment precisely.
Coca-Cola, Tesco own-label basics — broad appeal.

sections.content: **Mass vs niche** (3.1.5) is a common **evaluate** question. Match strategy to **firm size**, **resources**, and **product type** from the case — not every small firm should go niche, not every large firm should go mass.

items: **Mass:** High volume, low unit cost, national advertising, competitive pricing.
**Niche:** Specialist product, premium price, targeted promotion, selective distribution.
**Niche risk:** Market too small; copycat entrants.

sections.question: A small craft chocolate maker competes with global brands. Recommend mass or niche marketing and justify.
sections.solution:
**Recommend niche marketing.**

**Reasons:** Small firm lacks **budget** for mass TV advertising; product is **differentiated** (artisan, local ingredients) suited to **premium pricing**; can build **loyal local following** and selective stockists.

**Mass would fail:** Cannot match **economies of scale** or price of global rivals; generic messaging would **hide** unique selling points.

**Mix fit:** Premium **product**, high **price**, targeted **promotion** (food festivals, Instagram), selective **place** (delis, online subscription).
